artifacts.summary: Research on .opencode/ directory structure differences from .claude/ and what needs to be created/updated to mirror xlsx skill support
4-phase implementation plan for copying xlsx extension source files to nvim and creating .opencode/ variants with agent registration

memory_candidates.content: The extension loader uses config.lua presets to map .claude/ paths to .opencode/ equivalents. Key differences: agents go to agent/subagents/ (not agents/), config file is AGENTS.md (not CLAUDE.md), section prefix is extension_oc_ (not extension_), and merge_target_key is opencode_md (not claudemd).

When mirroring .claude/ extension files to .opencode/, the key path adjustments are: (1) .claude/context/formats/ becomes .opencode/context/core/formats/, (2) .claude/agents/ becomes .opencode/agent/subagents/, (3) .claude/skills/ stays .opencode/skills/. The opencode-agents.json exists in both .claude/ and .opencode/ extension directories and both need updating.

Implementation summary for xlsx filetypes extension integration

memory_candidates.content: When creating new skills/agents/commands for an extension, the files must be placed in the SOURCE extension directory (e.g., ~/.config/nvim/.claude/extensions/{name}/) not just in the target project. The extension loader installs from source to target; files created only in the target will be lost on re-sync and won't be available to other projects.

When extension files are created directly in a project, they must also be copied to the source extension directory to ensure other projects can load them. After placing files in the source, update the project extensions.json, context/index.json, and CLAUDE.md to register them.

artifacts.summary: Research report documenting thin-wrapper skill pattern, agent pattern, budget-agent xlsx workflow, extension registration structure, and implementation recommendations for skill-xlsx and xlsx-agent

memory_candidates.content: Filetypes extension thin-wrapper skill pattern: frontmatter has exactly 3 fields (name, description, allowed-tools: Task). Structure is always: Context Pointers (lazy ref to subagent-return.md), Trigger Conditions (direct/implicit/when-not), Execution (5 steps: validate -> context prep -> invoke agent via Task -> return validation -> propagation), Return Format, Error Handling. Agent invocation MUST use Task tool, never Skill tool.

Filetypes agents use simpler frontmatter than domain agents: only name and description (no model: or mcp-servers: fields). Their structure is: Overview, Agent Metadata (4 bullets), Allowed Tools (File Ops + Execution), Context References (@-refs), Supported Operations table, Execution Flow (6 stages: parse -> validate -> detect tools -> execute -> validate output -> return JSON), Error Handling, Critical Requirements (MUST DO / MUST NOT).

Filetypes extension skills follow a rigid thin-wrapper pattern: frontmatter with allowed-tools: Task, context pointer to subagent-return.md (lazy), trigger conditions (direct/implicit/exclusions), 5-step execution (validate -> context prep -> Task tool invoke -> return validate -> propagate), return format, error handling, MUST NOT section. Agents follow: frontmatter without model:/mcp-servers:, 6-stage execution (parse -> validate -> detect tools -> execute -> validate output -> return JSON). The closest analogs for new skills are skill-docx-edit (create/edit/analyze scope) and filetypes-spreadsheet-agent (same format domain).

active_projects.completion_summary: Fixed two keybinding bugs (ctrl-shift-a in Editor, secondary-shift-c in Workspace), removed redundant indent/outdent blocks from keymap.json, and rewrote Typst cheat sheet and markdown guide for macOS-only notation (Cmd/Opt instead of Ctrl/Cmd/Alt).

memory_candidates.content: Zed keybinding architecture on macOS: Zed defaults use cmd- internally but keymap.json uses secondary- for platform-adaptive bindings (Cmd on macOS, Ctrl on Linux). Custom bindings that need fixed Ctrl use explicit ctrl- prefix. When documenting for macOS-only, resolve secondary- to Cmd, keep ctrl- as Ctrl, and use Opt instead of Alt. Zed keeps Ctrl+G, Ctrl+Tab, and Ctrl+` as Ctrl even on macOS (not Cmd).
